ProcedureName: CT Chest Abdomen Pelvis with contrast
ReportText:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: Rising beta-hCG after evacuation of complete hydatidiform mole. Evaluate for metastatic gestational trophoblastic neoplasia.

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed after administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:
Lungs: Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules identified:
- Right lower lobe: 1.8 cm well-circumscribed nodule (dominant lesion)
- Right upper lobe: 0.9 cm nodule
- Right middle lobe: 0.6 cm nodule
- Left lower lobe: 1.2 cm and 0.7 cm nodules
- Left upper lobe: 0.8 cm, 0.5 cm, and 0.4 cm nodules
Total: 8 pulmonary nodules. Several nodules demonstrate a peripheral halo of ground-glass opacity, suggestive of hemorrhagic metastases (characteristic of choriocarcinoma).
No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.
Mediastinum: No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.
Heart: Normal size. No pericardial effusion.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: No focal hepatic lesions. No metastases.
Gallbladder and biliary: Unremarkable.
Pancreas: Normal.
Spleen: Normal.
Adrenals: Normal bilaterally.
Kidneys: Normal bilaterally. No hydronephrosis.
No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.
No ascites.

PELVIS:
Uterus: Heterogeneously enhancing mass within the endometrial cavity/myometrium measuring 3.2 x 2.8 x 3.5 cm with areas of necrosis and prominent surrounding vascularity. Findings consistent with intrauterine trophoblastic disease. Myometrial invasion appears to extend to the outer half of the myometrium.
Bilateral theca lutein cysts: Right ovary 3.8 cm, left ovary 2.4 cm (decreased from prior US).
Bladder: Normal.
No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bilateral pulmonary nodules (8 total, largest 1.8 cm right lower lobe) consistent with metastatic gestational trophoblastic neoplasia. Hemorrhagic halos suggest choriocarcinoma.
2. Heterogeneous intrauterine mass (3.2 cm) with deep myometrial invasion and prominent vascularity, consistent with choriocarcinoma.
3. Bilateral theca lutein cysts, decreasing.
4. No hepatic, renal, or other extrapulmonary metastases.
5. No brain imaging performed on this study - recommend dedicated brain MRI for complete staging.

ProcedureName: MRI Brain with and without contrast
ReportText:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: Staging for gestational trophoblastic neoplasia (choriocarcinoma) with known lung metastases. Rule out brain metastases.

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar multisequence MRI of the brain performed before and after administration of 15 mL Gadavist IV contrast. Sequences: T1, T2, FLAIR, DWI, SWI, post-contrast T1 (axial, coronal, sagittal).

FINDINGS:
Brain parenchyma: No enhancing lesions. No restricted diffusion. No hemorrhagic foci on SWI. Normal gray-white matter differentiation. No mass effect or midline shift. No cerebral edema.
Ventricles: Normal in size and configuration. No hydrocephalus.
Extra-axial spaces: Normal. No extra-axial collections.
Posterior fossa: Normal cerebellum and brainstem. No lesions.
Sella/pituitary: Normal.
Vessels: Normal flow voids in the major intracranial arteries. No aneurysm.
Calvarium and skull base: Normal marrow signal. No osseous lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1. No evidence of intracranial metastatic disease.
2. Normal brain MRI.
3. Recommend repeat brain MRI in 4-6 weeks given high-risk GTN with lung metastases.

PELVIC ULTRASOUND WITH DOPPLER

CLINICAL INDICATION: Post-molar GTN with rising hCG. Evaluate uterine disease.

TECHNIQUE: Transabdominal and transvaginal ultrasound with color Doppler.

FINDINGS:
Uterus: Anteverted, enlarged measuring 10.2 x 6.8 x 7.4 cm. Heterogeneous echogenic mass within the endometrial cavity extending into the myometrium, measuring 2.8 x 2.5 x 3.0 cm. The mass demonstrates markedly increased vascularity on color Doppler with low-resistance arterial flow (RI 0.38). The mass appears to invade the outer half of the myometrium. No definite serosal extension. Endometrial stripe is obliterated by the mass.

Right ovary: Enlarged at 4.0 x 3.2 cm with multiple anechoic cystic spaces consistent with theca lutein cysts (decreased from prior, previously 6.2 cm).
Left ovary: 2.6 x 2.0 cm, residual theca lutein cysts (previously 4.8 cm).

No free fluid in the cul-de-sac.

IMPRESSION:
1. Hypervascular intrauterine mass (2.8 cm) with deep myometrial invasion and low-resistance flow on Doppler, consistent with gestational trophoblastic neoplasia (choriocarcinoma). No definite serosal breach.
2. Bilateral theca lutein cysts, decreasing in size.
3. No free pelvic fluid.

ProcedureName: MRI Brain with and without contrast - follow-up
ReportText:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST - FOLLOW-UP

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-risk GTN (choriocarcinoma, FIGO Stage III, WHO score 11) on EMA-CO chemotherapy. Follow-up brain MRI per protocol.

COMPARISON: MRI Brain 2025-12-18

FINDINGS:
Brain parenchyma: No new or interval enhancing lesions. No restricted diffusion. No hemorrhagic foci. Normal appearance.
Ventricles: Unchanged, normal.
Extra-axial spaces: Normal.
Posterior fossa: Normal.

IMPRESSION:
1. No evidence of intracranial metastatic disease, unchanged from prior study.
2. Continue surveillance per protocol.

CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST - RESTAGING

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-risk GTN (choriocarcinoma), FIGO Stage III, on EMA-CO cycle 4. Restaging to assess treatment response.

COMPARISON: CT Chest 2025-12-15

FINDINGS:
CHEST:
Lungs: Interval marked decrease in pulmonary metastases:
- Right lower lobe: 0.8 cm (previously 1.8 cm)
- Right upper lobe: 0.3 cm (previously 0.9 cm)
- Right middle lobe: Not visualized (previously 0.6 cm, resolved)
- Left lower lobe: 0.5 cm (previously 1.2 cm); second nodule not visualized (previously 0.7 cm, resolved)
- Left upper lobe: 0.4 cm (previously 0.8 cm); two smaller nodules resolved
Total remaining: 4 nodules (previously 8). All significantly decreased. No new nodules.
No pleural effusions.
Mediastinum: No lymphadenopathy.

IMPRESSION:
1. Excellent treatment response with marked interval decrease in bilateral pulmonary metastases. Four of 8 nodules have resolved; remaining 4 are significantly smaller.
2. No new pulmonary disease.
3. Findings consistent with partial response to EMA-CO chemotherapy by RECIST criteria (>30% decrease in sum of target lesion diameters).
